write a python function to determine if a given musical note sequence can be sung within a specific vocal range. the input will be a list of strings, where each string represents a musical note (e.g., "c4", "g5", etc.), and two strings representing the lower and upper bounds of the singer's vocal range (e.g., "a3" and "f5"). the function should return a boolean value: true if all notes in the sequence fall within the specified vocal range, and false otherwise.

the function should parse each note string to extract the pitch and octave, then compare the pitch to the lower and upper bounds. the pitch is determined by the letter (c, d, e, f, g, a, b) and the octave (4, 5, etc.). the function should check if all notes are within the specified range and return true or false accordingly.

the function should be efficient and handle any valid note string input. it should also handle cases where the lower or upper bounds are not valid pitches or octaves. the function should return true only if all notes are within the specified range and false otherwise.

the function should be written in python and should take the list of notes, lower_bound, and upper_bound as input parameters. it should return a boolean value indicating whether the entire sequence of notes can be sung within the specified range.

the function should be well-documented and include comments explaining its purpose and the logic it uses. it should also handle edge cases, such as when the lower_bound is higher than the upper_bound or when the note sequence is empty. the function should return true only if all notes are within the specified range and false otherwise.

the function should be tested with the provided examples to ensure it works correctly. the function should also be able to handle other test cases, such as notes in different octaves or notes outside the specified range. the function should return true only if all notes are within the specified range and false otherwise.
